content:
# Final Review Arbiter
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Rejected fr-3-510c6e8d as a duplicate of the already-accepted fr-3-228f4433. Both amendments identify the same problem — the background_reap_timeout_drops_handle test manually replicates spawn_background_reap_with_timeout internals rather than calling the production function — but fr-3-228f4433 already covers this concern with concrete remediation options. Two of three reviewers (planner + reviewer 0) flagged the duplication. The one dissenting accept (reviewer 1) acknowledged the heavy overlap but voted accept on standalone technical merit; however, creating redundant work items for the same fix is not justified.

## Rulings

- `fr-3-510c6e8d`: Reject
  The technical observation is correct — the test at lines 4145-4179 does not call spawn_background_reap_with_timeout and instead replicates its internal timeout+drop logic. However, this is substantively identical to the already-accepted amendment fr-3-228f4433, which raises the same concern with more thorough alternatives (integration test calling the real function, doc-obligation fallback). The planner and one reviewer both independently identified this as a duplicate, and accepting both would create redundant remediation work targeting the exact same gap. The underlying issue is already addressed by fr-3-228f4433; a single remediation pass will cover what both amendments request.
